{{Quote|Never figured I rated high enough for the Emperor to notice. I'm just a guy making a living—your no-fly zones always got in the way.|Nas Ghent|<ref name="Walking the Path That's Given">{{Tales|21|Walking the Path That's Given}}</ref>}}
'''Nas Ghent''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and hotshot [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] who originally fought for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Confederate Navy/Legends|fleet]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
[[File:Nas_Ghent_TIE_pilot.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Nas Ghent wearing a [[TIE pilot]] flight suit]]

Ghent fought with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Confederate Navy/Legends|fleet]] at the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]] and was a decorated hero. After the war he turned to smuggling, taking dangerous runs through no-fly zones established by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] aboard his ship, the ''[[Rhodi's Silence]]''. He did so in an attempt to find a way to cope with the regrets he had from his past and to recapture something of the thrill he had felt in his youth while flying for the Separatists.

On one such run, he was shot out of space by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Ghent figured his time had come and accepted his death. However, Vader deactivated his lightsaber and said he was not going to kill him. Vader said that his aviation skills were impressive, but that he is wasting his skills smuggling, and personally recruited Ghent to recruit a crack Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] unit, [[Black Eight Squadron]]. Assigned aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Crucible (Imperial-class)|''Crucible'']], the flagship of an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], Ghent clashed with the ambitious deck officer [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Dorin Millavec]]. Millavec had been nearing the end of his captaincy of the ''Crucible'' and awaiting a career-enhancing move into Lower Command, when he had been sidelined by Darth Vader. Vader ordered Ghent to find seven individuals with unorthodox thinking methods to be assigned to the ''Crucible'', even authorizing Millavec to pull them out of the Imperial prisons if he saw fit. Once brought aboard, Ghent would be responsible for all flying and training. Ghent would report only to Millavec, who in turn would report only to Vader. Millavec balked at Vader's extension of his assignment aboard the ''Crucible'', but Vader bluntly told Millavec he was staying onboard ship until Vader was personally pleased with Ghent's squadron, and also warned Millavec not to test the likes of Darth Vader, lest he be sent to a remote listening post on the Outer Rim, which would be sufficient to doom an Imperial career, or even be sent to the Emperor's throne room for an even worse fate.

Meanwhile, Ghent was prepared for his job as a squadron commander: by having his vitals tested and his long hair shaved into a buzzcut, and being issued the uniform of an Imperial Sub-Lieutenant.

An annoyed Millavec decided to get rid of the "upstart" by arranging for him to be killed in a supposed accident: On his first familiarization flight, Ghent was to be given a decrepit TIE fighter and to be shot from behind by his flight members as a rebel infiltrator. However, despite the condition of his ship, Ghent managed to eradicate his two wingmen and crash land into the landing bay, which astonished the crew of the ''Crucible''. An enraged Ghent said if Millavec has a problem with him, he needs to try and kill him face-to-face instead of playing games like a coward. Dorin Millavec coolly replied that the attack was a test from Darth Vader to prove his worthiness as a squad leader, and that Ghent passed with flying colors. Ghent, however, did not appear assuaged by this.

Later, with his second-in-command, Millavec remarked that Ghent simply needs another assassination attempt, and sought to do so through seven murderous prisoners he had released from prison. The XO remarked that Ghent may wise up to the fact that Vader did not order the hit, but Millavec smugly responds he has his bases covered in that the trainees will kill Ghent, and Vader will forget about his plans through his obsession with finding that [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Rebel pilot who destroyed the Death Star]].

This vicious plot, however, only served to re-kindle Ghent's fighting spirit, and he silently vowed to himself to mold this squadron, called the Black Eight, into the Empire's finest, and to get even with Millavec.

==Behind the scenes==
The story of Ghent was left on a cliffhanger, as the formation of the squadron was never depicted. However, [[Jeremy Barlow]] suggested that the story may be continued in a future [[Star Wars: Rebellion (comic series)|''Rebellion'']] arc, and has hinted that [[BoShek/Legends|BoShek]] will be one pilot recruited by Ghent into the Black Eight Squadron.<ref>{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?t=9774&start=0|thread=Nas Ghent's story|user=[[Jeremy Barlow]]|date=2006-08-07|time=12:13 PM}}</ref>
